ogTitle: McGraths Hill vs McKees Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McGraths Hill and McKees Hill.
heading: McGraths Hill vs McKees Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McGraths Hill, New South Wales and McKees Hill, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McKees Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to McGraths Hill (51/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McGraths Hill or McKees Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McGraths Hill has a flood risk score of 58/100, while McKees Hill scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McGraths Hill scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. McKees Hill scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McGraths Hill or McKees Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McKees Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 3.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
